title: Biology As Enemy
definition: The recognition that our biology can be both our worst enemy and the vehicle for transcendence.

difficulty_levels.beginner: The idea that our body, with its thoughts and feelings, often keeps us from seeing the truth, but can also help us understand it if we know how to look.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: An insight into the dual nature of biology: as both a barrier to understanding and a conduit for transcendence through the recognition of its limits.
difficulty_levels.expert: The acknowledgement that our neurophysiological machinery, with its capacity for self-reflection and narrative construction, can create illusions and prevent us from seeing reality as it is, but can also help us perceive the truth by understanding its workings.

extra_fields.insight: Understanding that our biology is not inherently bad or good, but a tool that we can learn to use or let go of as needed in the pursuit of truth and transcendence.
